title: Vagus Nerve Collection and Histology Microscopy Protocol
author: J. Ashley Ezzell
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.6bqhamw
materials: We rinsed each sample with deionized water.
We rinsed the slides in distilled water.

steps.title: We collected fresh vagus nerve samples...
steps.instruction: We collected fresh vagus nerve samples from Yorkshire pigs after they were euthanized following medical training courses at Duke University.

steps.title: We collected samples of the carotid...
steps.instruction: We collected samples of the carotid sheath in the neck bilaterally. We collected 2 cm samples at the level midway along the rostocaudal line from the rostral end of the sternum to the line connecting the left and right angles of the mandible (typically ~12 to 14 cm apart). We measured from the "valley" of the common carotid bifurcation to the center of each sample.

steps.title: We collected ~2 cm samples of...
steps.instruction: We collected ~2 cm samples of the anterior and posterior subdiaphragmatic vagus nerve trunks along the esophagus approximately halfway between the diaphragmatic esophageal hiatus and the gastroesophageal junction.

steps.title: We dyed the rostral end of...
steps.instruction: We dyed the rostral end of each sample green to maintain orientation during processing.

steps.title: We placed each sample between two...
steps.instruction: We placed each sample between two histology sponges in a mega-sized histology cassette. We placed the cassettes in a tub with 4% paraformaldehyde in a 4 o C refrigerator.

steps.title: b
steps.instruction: b.We perfused the rats with ~300 mL 1x PBS oruntil the liver was cleared, followed by ~300 mL 4% paraformaldehyde or untilthe joints and tissues were stiff.

steps.title: c
steps.instruction: c.We collected samples of the carotid sheath inthe neck bilaterally, where the vagus nerve courses straight with the commoncarotid artery, from the clavicle up to where the nerve and carotid interminglewith non-parallel other nerves. We measured from the "valley" of thecommon carotid bifurcation to the center of each sample.

steps.title: d
steps.instruction: d.We collected the esophagus from the diaphragm tothe stomach with the subdiaphragmatic vagus nerves attached.

steps.title: e
steps.instruction: e.We dyed the rostral end of each sample green tomaintain orientation during processing.

steps.title: f
steps.instruction: f.We placed each sample between two histologysponges in a mega-sized histology cassette. We placed the cassettes in a tubwith 4% paraformaldehyde in a 4 o C fridge.

steps.title: Each sample was cut in half...
steps.instruction: Each sample was cut in half along its length andembedded in a single paraffin block with the two central surfaces of the nerveexposed for the initial slices.

steps.title: 5 µmslices were taken and placed on microscope slides
steps.instruction: 5 µmslices were taken and placed on microscope slides.[NP1] The slides were stained with Masson’s trichrome.

steps.title: 0pt'>[NP1]Couldprovide more details on the slicing...
steps.instruction: 0pt'>[NP1]Couldprovide more details on the slicing (took 30 slices, mounted on slides in thisorder, stained slides ## and ## with MT, …), but perhaps too much detail?

steps.title: We perfused the rats with ~300...
steps.instruction: We perfused the rats with ~300 mL 1x PBS oruntil the liver was cleared, followed by ~300 mL 4% paraformaldehyde or untilthe joints and tissues were stiff.

steps.title: We collected samples of the carotid...
steps.instruction: We collected samples of the carotid sheath inthe neck bilaterally, where the vagus nerve courses straight with the commoncarotid artery, from the clavicle up to where the nerve and carotid interminglewith non-parallel other nerves. We measured from the "valley" of thecommon carotid bifurcation to the center of each sample.

steps.title: We collected the esophagus from the...
steps.instruction: We collected the esophagus from the diaphragm tothe stomach with the subdiaphragmatic vagus nerves attached.

steps.title: We dyed the rostral end of...
steps.instruction: We dyed the rostral end of each sample green tomaintain orientation during processing.We placed each sample between two histologysponges in a mega-sized histology cassette. We placed the cassettes in a tubwith 4% paraformaldehyde in a 4 o C fridge.

steps.title: Each sample was imaged at 10x...
steps.instruction: Each sample was imaged at 10x using a Nikon Ti2 microscope with a Photometrics Prime 95B-25MM camera (Nikon Instruments Inc.). We selected the best of four slices for each sample based on the quality of the slice (no tearing or fraying).

steps.title: We processed each sample on the...
steps.instruction: We processed each sample on the long cycle in the Leica ASP300S Tissue Processor for ~10 hours: 70, 80, 95, 95, 100, 100,100% ethanol for 30, 35, 40, 40, 40, 40, 40 min, respectively; Clearite for 50min, three times; paraffin wax for 50 min, three times.

steps.title: We cut each sample in half...
steps.instruction: We cut each sample in half transversely and embedded the halves together cut side down in paraffin in order to obtain transverse sections starting from the center of the nerve and moving outward.

steps.title: We collected 5 µm sections, placing...
steps.instruction: We collected 5 µm sections, placing two serial sections per microscope slide for fifteen slides.

steps.title: The slides were air dried overnight...
steps.instruction: The slides were air dried overnight at room temperature, then baked at 37 o C overnight.

steps.title: Of the 15 slides per sample,...
steps.instruction: Of the 15 slides per sample, we stained slides 2 and 14 with Masson's trichrome as follows.

steps.title: The slides were baked at 60 o C for 1
steps.instruction: The slides were baked at 60 o C for 1.5 hours and then cooled overnight.

steps.title: We deparaffinized the slides and hydrated...
steps.instruction: We deparaffinized the slides and hydrated them to distilled water: xylene (2x 6 min), 100% ethanol (5 min), 95% ethanol (4 min), 70% ethanol (3 min), dH2O (2x 1 min).

steps.title: We placed the slides in Bouin's...
steps.instruction: We placed the slides in Bouin's fixative (mordant) at room temperature overnight.

steps.title: We washed the slides in running...
steps.instruction: We washed the slides in running tap water until the yellow color disappeared (~10 minutes).

steps.title: We placed the slides in Weigert's...
steps.instruction: We placed the slides in Weigert's Iron Hematoxylin solution for 10 min.

steps.title: We washed the slides in running tap water for 10min
steps.instruction: We washed the slides in running tap water for 10min.

steps.title: We placed the slides in Biebrich...
steps.instruction: We placed the slides in Biebrich Scarlet-Acid Fuchsin solution for 5 min.

steps.title: We washed the slides in running...
steps.instruction: We washed the slides in running tap water for 2 min.

steps.title: We placed the slides in Phosphomolybdic-Phosphotungstic...
steps.instruction: We placed the slides in Phosphomolybdic-Phosphotungstic Acid solution for 10 min.

steps.title: We transferred the slides directly to...
steps.instruction: We transferred the slides directly to Aniline Blue solution for 3 min.

steps.title: We differentiated the counterstain (A
steps.instruction: We differentiated the counterstain (A. blue) in 1% Acetic Acid solution for 1 min.

steps.title: We dehydrated, cleared, and coverslipped the slides
steps.instruction: We dehydrated, cleared, and coverslipped the slides: 95% ethanol (1 min), 100% ethanol (4 min), xylene (5 min).
